mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 330) signals central bank intervention risks, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in rate hikes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 330 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL indicate tightening monetary policy against inflation, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days due to higher discount rates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.5pp and CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3pp in 1 month
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of cases where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise by thresholds, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 1500k) signal robust demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as construction activity drives economic growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where HOUST exceeds 1500 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 102.5) indicates improving supply-side capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as corporate earnings prospects improve.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 30% of cases where INDPRO exceeds 102.5, or Rank IC < 0.01 over 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment (UNRATE < 4.0%) signals tight labor markets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as wage pressures drive corporate profitability.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of instances where UNRATE falls below 4.0, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1 > 24174.5) and retail sales (RSAFS > 757085) indicates strong demand, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 rises > 0.5% and RSAFS rises > 0.3% in 1 quarter
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in > 40% of cases where both GDPC1 and RSAFS exceed thresholds, or Rank IC < 0.03 over 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 158736) indicates labor market strength, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as consumer spending accelerates.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 30% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 158736, or Rank IC < 0.01 over 1-year window.
